title: ludwigia stenorraphe subsp. speciosa
intro: Ludwigia stenorraphe subsp. speciosa, also known as Ludwigia speciosa or water primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Ludwigia stenorraphe subsp. speciosa is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its medicinal properties.
